name: QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - TLS Unrecommended Cipher Suite in Use
notes:
This rule triggers when QRadar Network Insights detects that either a TLS 1.3 or TLS 1.2 session is using a cipher suite that is not recommended by NIST publication 800-52r2.

Note: The QNI : TLS 1.3 Recommended Cipher Suites and  QNI : TLS 1.2 Recommended Cipher Suites reference sets have been prepopulated. Tune these Reference Sets with relevant cipher suites.
conditions_text: when the flow matches Protocol Name is not N/A, Protocol Name is any of TLS, Protocol Version is not N/A, Protocol Version is any of [1.2 or 1.3], SSL/TLS Cipher Suite is not N/A
when any of SSL/TLS Cipher Suite are contained in any of QNI : TLS 1.3 Recommended Cipher Suites - AlphaNumeric, QNI : TLS 1.2 Recommended Cipher Suites - AlphaNumeric
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - TLS Unrecommended Cipher Suite in Use on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the flow matches Protocol Name is not N/A, Protocol Name is any of TLS, Protocol Version is not N/A, Protocol Version is any of [1.2 or 1.3], SSL/TLS Cipher Suite is not N/A
AND NOT when any of SSL/TLS Cipher Suite are contained in any of QNI : TLS 1.3 Recommended Cipher Suites - AlphaNumeric, QNI : TLS 1.2 Recommended Cipher Suites - AlphaNumeric
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - TLS Unrecommended Cipher Suite in Use
Event description: Detects when either a TLS 1.3 or TLS 1.2 session has been observed using an unrecommended cipher suite.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
